program.name: Taiku 90-Day Program
program.philosophy: Protect easy days, pre-planned deloads, progressive overload via double progression, running is the priority.
program.progressionRule: Double progression: when you hit the top of the rep range on all sets at the prescribed RIR, increase the weight next session and drop back to the bottom of the range.
program.deloadRule: Deload weeks: same exercises, 60% of last build-week load, reduced sets. Do not skip, do not add.

weeks.label: Movement practice
weeks.note: Weights must feel too easy. You are greasing patterns, not training hard. Expect DOMS to hit your runs this week and next — it passes.

weeks.label: Movement practice
weeks.note: Small weight increases only if all reps were clean last week.

weeks.label: Movement practice
weeks.note: Last week of the easy block. DOMS should be mostly gone by now.

weeks.label: Deload 1
weeks.note: 60% load, 2 sets per exercise. This week is part of the plan, not a reward.

weeks.label: Real progression starts
weeks.note: RIR drops to 2. Optional exercise upgrades unlock (barbell squat, barbell RDL, loaded split squats).

weeks.label: Build
weeks.note: Follow double progression strictly. No extra sets on good days.

weeks.label: Build
weeks.note: Heaviest week so far. Watch how your easy runs feel — they are your dashboard.

weeks.label: Consolidation
weeks.note: Main lifts move to 6-8 reps, slightly heavier. Accessories stay at 8-12.

weeks.label: Consolidation
weeks.note: Keep RIR honest at 2. No grinding reps, ever.

weeks.label: Peak week
weeks.note: Heaviest week of the program. Protect sleep this week.

weeks.label: Final week
weeks.note: Repeat week 11 loads and enjoy how easy they feel compared to day 1. Then plan the next cycle.

run_jog.title: Easy run
run_jog.intent: Conversational pace. This is recovery, don't push. Details on Garmin.

run_interval.title: Interval run
run_interval.intent: The hard run of the week. Details on Garmin.

run_long.title: Long run
run_long.intent: Long and easy. Details on Garmin.

mobility.title: Mobility & active recovery
mobility.intent: Genuinely easy. This doubles as active recovery before the long run. No swings, no extras.

drills.name: Knee-to-wall ankle dorsiflexion (kettlebell on knee)
drills.note: Achilles and calf insurance. Slow, controlled.

drills.name: Couch stretch (hip flexors)
drills.note: The anti-sitting stretch. Squeeze the glute of the stretched side.

drills.name: 90/90 hip rotations
drills.note: No hands if possible. Smooth, no bouncing.

drills.name: Deep goblet squat hold (kettlebell)
drills.note: Use elbows to pry knees out. Stay tall.

drills.name: Thoracic openers (open books + cat-cow)
drills.note: Slow breathing, rotate from the upper back.

drills.name: Band pull-aparts + dislocates
drills.note: Light band, full range, finish relaxed.

gym_a.title: Gym A — Squat day

warmup.general: 6 min easy run to the gym
warmup.specific: Per-exercise ramp sets are built in

gym_a.cooldown: Easy 6 min run home as a flush

exercises.name: Bench press
exercises.note: Tight upper back, elbows ~45°. Touch chest, no bounce.

exercises.name: Row
exercises.note: Squeeze the shoulder blades back and down. No body swing.

gym_b.title: Gym B — Deadlift day

warmup.general: 6 min easy run to the gym
warmup.specific: Per-exercise ramp sets are built in

gym_b.cooldown: Easy 6 min run home as a flush

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.note: Bar over mid-foot. Brace, slack out, push the floor away.

exercises.name: Bench press
exercises.note: Tight upper back, elbows ~45°. Touch chest, no bounce.

exercises.name: Row
exercises.note: Squeeze the shoulder blades back and down. No body swing.
